verses.text: Moses and the elders commanded: keep all the commandments. When ye pass over Jordan, set up great stones, plaster them with plaster, write upon them all the words of this law. When thou hast passed over, that thou mayest go in unto the land. Guidance: The first thing to build on the new side of the river is not a fort but a billboard. Great stones, plaster, the whole Torah inscribed — set up where every traveler sees. The land's first piece of public art is God's words. Foundations matter.

Moses and the elders commanded: keep all the commandments. When ye pass over Jordan, set up great stones, plaster them with plaster, write upon them all the words of this law. When thou hast passed over, that thou mayest go in unto the land. Guidance: The first thing to build on the new side of the river is not a fort but a billboard. Great stones, plaster, the whole Torah inscribed — set up where every traveler sees. The land's first piece of public art is God's words. Foundations matter.

Set up these stones in mount Ebal, plaster with plaster. Build an altar unto the LORD of stones; lift up no iron tool upon them. Build of whole stones; offer burnt offerings and peace offerings, eat there, rejoice before the LORD thy God. Write upon the stones all the words of this law very plainly. Guidance: Stones uncut by human chisels, plaster smooth enough for ink. The altar is rough; the inscription is plain. Worship that doesn't try to improve God's words. Eat and rejoice on the same mountain where the curses will later echo — God's first directive on the conquered land is JOY.

Moses and the priests Levites spake unto all Israel: Take heed, hearken, O Israel; this day thou art become the people of the LORD thy God. Thou shalt therefore obey the voice of the LORD thy God, do His commandments. Guidance: 'This day thou ART become.' Not 'shall be' — already. The covenant transition happens by the renewed avowal. Identity precedes obedience, and obedience confirms identity. Today you became His; therefore walk like it.

Moses and the priests Levites spake unto all Israel: Take heed, hearken, O Israel; this day thou art become the people of the LORD thy God. Thou shalt therefore obey the voice of the LORD thy God, do His commandments. Guidance: 'This day thou ART become.' Not 'shall be' — already. The covenant transition happens by the renewed avowal. Identity precedes obedience, and obedience confirms identity. Today you became His; therefore walk like it.

Moses commanded: These shall stand upon mount Gerizim to bless the people: Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Joseph, Benjamin. These shall stand upon mount Ebal to curse: Reuben, Gad, Asher, Zebulun, Dan, Naphtali. Guidance: Six tribes facing six tribes across the valley, the priests and ark in the middle. Geography becomes liturgy. The blessing side and the curse side are equally peopled — the choice is real. God assigns tribes their position so no one is exempt from the public reckoning.

The Levites shall speak with a loud voice: Cursed be the man that maketh any graven image. Amen. Cursed that setteth light by his father or mother. Amen. That removeth his neighbour's landmark. Amen. That maketh the blind to wander. Amen. That perverteth the judgment of stranger, fatherless, widow. Amen. That lieth with his father's wife, with any beast, with his sister, with his mother in law. Amen. That smiteth his neighbour secretly. Amen. That taketh reward to slay innocent blood. Amen. That confirmeth not all the words of this law to do them. And all the people shall say, Amen. Guidance: Twelve curses, twelve hidden sins — images, parent-disgust, moved landmarks, blind-led-astray, biased courts, family incest, secret violence, secret bribes, secret unbelief. Each ends with the people's amen. The hardest sins to police are the secret ones; God names them out loud and asks the public to agree. To say amen is to put yourself under the verdict — and to refuse the sin.
